Project.name: Qwen3 TTS on Apple Silicon
Project.annotation: Local text-to-speech stack built around Qwen3 TTS models with three transport adapters (HTTP server, Telegram bot, interactive CLI) and a shared runtime core providing model registry, backend selection, job orchestration, and observability.
Project.keywords: TTS, text-to-speech, Qwen3, Apple Silicon, MLX, PyTorch, FastAPI, Telegram bot, CLI, voice synthesis, voice cloning, voice design, audio generation, model inference

Actors.actor-APIClient: External HTTP client consuming the FastAPI TTS endpoints.
Actors.actor-TelegramUser: Telegram user interacting via bot commands.
Actors.actor-CLIUser: Local operator using the interactive CLI for synthesis workflows.
Actors.actor-System: Internal runtime performing backend selection, model loading, and job execution.

UC-001.Action: Submits a text-to-speech request with a named speaker
UC-001.Goal: To receive synthesized audio in WAV format via HTTP response
UC-001.Preconditions: Server is running, at least one model is available
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: Audio bytes returned with correct Content-Type, generation log markers emitted

UC-002.Action: Submits a voice design request with a natural-language voice description
UC-002.Goal: To receive audio synthesized with a dynamically designed voice
UC-002.Preconditions: Server is running, VoiceDesign model is available
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: Audio bytes returned, design-mode log markers emitted

UC-003.Action: Submits a voice clone request with reference audio
UC-003.Goal: To receive audio synthesized in the cloned voice
UC-003.Preconditions: Server is running, CustomVoice model is available, reference audio is valid
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Audio returned, reference audio converted if needed, clone-mode log markers emitted

UC-004.Action: Submits an async TTS job and polls for result
UC-004.Goal: To receive a job ID immediately and retrieve the result later
UC-004.Preconditions: Server is running, job queue not full
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: Job ID returned on submit, status endpoint shows progress, result endpoint returns audio on completion

UC-005.Action: Sends /tts command with text and optional speaker/speed/language
UC-005.Goal: To receive a voice message with synthesized audio in the chat
UC-005.Preconditions: Bot is running, user is in allowlist, rate limit not exceeded
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: Voice message delivered to user, delivery metadata persisted

UC-006.Action: Sends /clone command replying to a voice/audio message
UC-006.Goal: To receive audio cloned from the reference voice
UC-006.Preconditions: Bot is running, user is in allowlist, reference audio is attached
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: Cloned voice message delivered, reference audio cleaned up

UC-007.Action: Runs interactive CLI and enters synthesis commands
UC-007.Goal: To synthesize and optionally play audio locally
UC-007.Preconditions: Python environment active, model files present in .models/
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Audio generated and optionally played via sounddevice

UC-008.Action: Selects and loads the appropriate backend (MLX or Torch) at startup
UC-008.Goal: To provide a ready inference backend without manual configuration
UC-008.Preconditions: At least one backend runtime is installed
UC-008.AcceptanceCriteria: Backend selected, readiness report shows available models

NonGoals.item-1: No multi-user cloud deployment or horizontal scaling in v1.
NonGoals.item-3: No webhook mode for Telegram bot.
NonGoals.item-4: No group chat or inline query support in Telegram.

Constraints.constraint-1: Must run on a single machine with local model files.
Constraints.constraint-2: Apple Silicon (MLX) is the primary target; PyTorch is the fallback.
Constraints.constraint-4: ffmpeg must be available in PATH for audio conversion.
Constraints.constraint-5: Secrets (tokens, credentials) must never appear in logs.
Constraints.constraint-6: All transport adapters must reuse the shared core runtime.

Risks.risk-1: Large model files may cause long startup times with preloading enabled.
Risks.risk-2: Single inference guard means concurrent requests queue behind each other.
Risks.risk-3: Telegram bot token leakage through logs or error messages.
Risks.risk-4: Audio conversion failures with unsupported formats in clone flows.

OpenQuestions.question-1: Should the async job system support persistent storage beyond in-memory?
OpenQuestions.question-2: Should streaming inference be exposed at the transport layer?
